We need to determine the falling distance using d = k*t^2. Let me find k from the examples.
I will put my final answer inside \boxed{}.

t = 3.68s, d = 40.06m:
t^2 = 3.68 * 3.68:
3.68 * 0.08 = 0.2944
3.68 * 0.60 = 2.2080
3.68 * 3.00 = 11.0400
0.2944 + 2.2080 = 2.5024
2.5024 + 11.0400 = 13.5424
k = 40.06 / 3.68^2 = 40.06 / 13.5424 = 40.060 / 13.542
= 0 + 1 * 40060 / 13542
= 1 + 1 * 26518 / 13542
= 2 + 1 * 12976 / 13542
= 2.0 + 0.1 * 129760 / 13542
= 2.1 + 0.1 * 116218 / 13542
= 2.2 + 0.1 * 102676 / 13542

= 2.4 + 0.1 * 75592 / 13542
= 2.5 + 0.1 * 62050 / 13542
= 2.6 + 0.1 * 48508 / 13542
= 2.7 + 0.1 * 34966 / 13542
= 2.8 + 0.1 * 21424 / 13542
= 2.9 + 0.1 * 7882 / 13542
= 2.90 + 0.01 * 78820 / 13542
= 2.91 + 0.01 * 65278 / 13542
= 2.92 + 0.01 * 51736 / 13542
= 2.93 + 0.01 * 38194 / 13542
= 2.94 + 0.01 * 24652 / 13542
= 2.95 + 0.01 * 11110 / 13542
= 2.950 + 0.001 * 111100 / 13542

= 2.953 + 0.001 * 70474 / 13542

= 2.958 + 0.001 * 2764 / 13542
= 2.958

For t = 3.84:
t^2 = 3.84 * 3.84:
3.84 * 0.04 = 0.1536
3.84 * 0.80 = 3.0720
3.84 * 3.00 = 11.5200
0.1536 + 3.0720 = 3.2256
3.2256 + 11.5200 = 14.7456
= 14.7456

d = 2.958 * 14.7456:
2.958 * 0.0006 = 0.0017748
2.958 * 0.0050 = 0.0147900
2.958 * 0.0400 = 0.1183200
2.958 * 0.7000 = 2.0706000
2.958 * 4.0000 = 11.8320000
2.958 * 10.0000 = 29.5800000
0.0017748 + 0.0147900 = 0.0165648
0.0165648 + 0.1183200 = 0.1348848
0.1348848 + 2.0706000 = 2.2054848
2.2054848 + 11.8320000 = 14.0374848
14.0374848 + 29.5800000 = 43.6174848
= 43.617

I will now return the answer in \boxed{}
